renderControls.thresholdTooltip: Minimum number of shaded points to consider a zone as shaded

simulationControls.title: Annual Simulation
simulationControls.thresholdTooltip: Minimum number of shaded points to consider a zone as shaded
simulationControls.stopConfirm: Stop the simulation? Progress will be lost for setups still running.

simulationResultsPanel.title: Annual Results
simulationResultsPanel.placeholder: Run the annual simulation to see results here. Use the controls on the left to configure the interval, year, and irradiance source, then press RUN CALCULATION.

resultsPanel.title: Results
resultsPanel.noActiveSetups: No setups selected. Click a setup in the legend to show it.

report.generateTitle: Generate a PDF report for the selected simulation run
report.modalDescription: The report always includes annual and monthly production data and shadow heat maps for all setups. Optionally add representative days to include hourly production curves for specific dates (e.g. one day per season).

angleWarning.title: Configuration Warning
angleWarning.message: This application only supports 90°/180° wall angles. The following sets of points form angles that do not meet this requirement and rendering may be inaccurate:

opfsError.title: Browser not supported
opfsError.message: This application requires the Origin Private File System (OPFS) API to store your configuration between sessions. Your current browser does not support it. Please update to a recent version of one of the following browsers:

exportImport.exportTitle: Export the current configuration and its simulation results to a backup file
exportImport.importTitle: Load a backup file to restore configuration and simulation results
exportImport.importDescription: Loading a backup replaces the current configuration and all simulation results. Open-Meteo irradiance data in the local cache is preserved.

configuration.firstLaunchMessage: This is the built-in example configuration. Edit it to match your installation, or load your own config.json file using the buttons below.
configuration.invalidConfig: The file does not contain a valid configuration.
configuration.exportFileTitle: Download the current configuration as config.json
configuration.importFileTitle: Load a config.json file and apply it immediately
configuration.resetTitle: Clear the saved configuration and reload the built-in default
configuration.resetConfirm: Reset to the default configuration? The current configuration will be replaced by the built-in example.
configuration.changeWarning: Changing the configuration will make existing simulation results inaccessible in the results panel, because those results are linked to the previous configuration. You can keep them in storage and recover them by reloading the previous configuration, or delete them now to free up space.
configuration.docLinkMessage: Need help configuring your installation? Check the documentation:
